channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Six suspects, including KDF officers, detained over alleged plot to attack Major General
item.description: A Nairobi court has allowed police to hold two Kenya Defence Forces (KDF) officers and four civilians for 10 days as investigations continue into an alleged plot to rob and kill Major General George O...

item.title: US Lawmakers Seek Transparency on Iran Conflict, Praise Pakistan’s Mediation Role
item.description: Democrats on the US House Armed Services Committee have demanded a public hearing regarding President Donald Trump’s ongoing war with Iran. The lawmakers, led by Adam Smith and supported by all Democr...

item.title: Pakistan Holds High-Level Consultations Amid Gulf Conflict
item.description: With the ongoing war on Pakistan’s borders, the government is taking serious steps to safeguard national interests. On Thursday, President Asif Zardari chaired a high-level meeting attended by the Pri...

item.title: Controversy as KWS clears 76 acres in Nairobi Park for new animal orphanage and Bomas expansion
item.description: Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) has started clearing 76 acres of forest land inside Nairobi National Park to relocate the 62-year-old Nairobi Animal Orphanage and support expansion of the Sh41.9 billion ...

item.title: Pakistan allows easier land exports of food and medicines to Iran
item.description: Amid growing uncertainties in maritime trade due to the ongoing Gulf crisis, Pakistan has announced a temporary relaxation in its trade rules with Iran. The government has allowed exporters to ship te...

item.title: Tanzania launches 2.6bn/- climate resilience project for vulnerable communities
item.description: Dodoma: The Vice-President’s Office in Tanzania has earmarked 2.6bn/- to support a climate resilience project targeting communities and ecosystems, particularly in the miombo woodland zones and some a...

item.title: How the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill 2026 Undermines Self-Identification and Trans Rights
item.description: The Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, recently passed in the Rajya Sabha, introduces stricter verification rules for transgender identity in the name of preventing misus...

item.title: Tshwane Council Starts Disciplinary Process Against CFO Over Tender Allegations
item.description: The City of Tshwane has initiated disciplinary proceedings against its chief financial officer, Gareth Mnisi, following allegations that he shared sensitive tender information with a police officer in...

item.title: UN Security Council discusses civilian casualties in Iran school bombing
item.description: The United Nations Security Council (UNSC) convened a closed-door meeting in New York on Friday at Russia’s request to address the recent US-Israeli airstrikes on civilian sites in Iran. Diplomats inf...

item.title: Supporting Entrepreneurs in South Africa: Strategy Meets Reality
item.description: Sarah Mthintso, executive director of the SAB Foundation, discusses the work of the foundation in supporting South African entrepreneurs. The SAB Foundation focuses on entrepreneurs and innovators who...

item.title: ECOWAS Court launches 2026–2030 plan to strengthen justice and human rights across West Africa
item.description: The ECOWAS Court of Justice don launch im new five-year Strategic Plan wey go guide di court operations from 2026 to 2030. Di plan, wey dem name 'Justice 2030', aim to make di court stronger for regio...

item.title: Tinubu Affirms Support for Credible Opposition, Rejects One-Party State Claims
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don talk say e dey believe in make we get credible opposition wey fit challenge government, question policies and make dem better. E come reject talk wey dey say APC wan tu...

item.title: Tanzania urged to focus on investment-driven trade as global aid model evolves
item.description: As global trade becomes more fragmented and international funding tightens, Tanzania and other least developed countries are being called to shift from relying on aid to building competitive investmen...

item.title: Pakistan secures IMF staff-level agreement for $1.2bn under ongoing loan programme
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the Government of Pakistan have reached a staff-level agreement (SLA) for the release of about $1.2 billion in funding. This agreement follows the successful ...

item.title: Reform UK candidate Corey Edwards withdraws from Welsh Senedd election after controversial photograph
item.description: A Reform UK candidate for the Welsh Senedd elections scheduled for May has stepped down from his campaign. Corey Edwards, the party's lead candidate for the Pen-y-bont Bro Morgannwg constituency and a...

item.title: US President Urges Iran to Reopen Strategic Strait
item.description: The United States President has called on Iran to reopen a strategically important waterway, which he referred to as the 'Strait of Trump'. This statement comes amid ongoing tensions in the region ove...

item.title: Rayvanny’s ‘Tetema’: A Lasting Symbol of East Africa’s Streaming Growth
item.description: The article by Beatrice Materu explores the long-lasting influence of Rayvanny’s hit song “Tetema,” featuring Diamond Platnumz, in shaping East Africa’s music streaming story. Released in 2019, “Tetem...

item.title: Lahore High Court annuls 80‑year‑old inheritance mutation based on fake will among Muslim heirs
item.description: In Lahore, the Lahore High Court has partially allowed a civil revision and set aside an 80‑year‑old inheritance mutation that was based on what it called a fictitious and fraudulent will. The dispute...

item.title: Tanzania’s Daily Struggles with Unreliable Electricity Supply
item.description: This opinion piece by Beatrice Materu explores Tanzania’s complicated and emotional relationship with electricity. While the national slogan promises to 'light up your present and future life', the re...

item.title: Provinces Resist Sharing Fuel Subsidy Costs Amid Rising Global Oil Prices
item.description: Provinces in Pakistan, especially Punjab and Sindh, are reluctant to bear the Rs200 billion fuel subsidy burden asked by the federal government to shield consumers from global oil price hikes. They fa...
